hours_formula_detail: Alaska uses the flat federal baseline: ABAWDs must work, participate in an approved work program, or perform community service / volunteer at a public, quasi-public, or nonprofit organization for a total of 80 hours or more during a 30-day period (equivalently 20 hours/week). No SNAP-allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage (workfare) formula is used for the general community-service volunteer lane. Alaska's FNS-approved FY26 SNAP E&T State Plan (read in full in prior research) does NOT check the Workfare component box, so the comparable-workfare formula does not apply. The generic 'allotment / minimum wage' formula that appears in national explainers describes the optional state Workfare component, which Alaska does not operate. Authority for counting general unpaid volunteer hours: 7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii).
minimum_wage_context: Not used in the hours formula because Alaska is federal_80 (flat 80 hrs/month). For reference only: Alaska statewide minimum wage is $13.00/hour effective Jan 1, 2026, rising to $14.00/hour effective July 1, 2026 (Ballot Measure 1, Nov 2024). Higher of state vs federal $7.25 would be $13.00-$14.00 if a workfare formula were ever applied, but Alaska does not run one.
enforcement_detail: ABAWD time limit is actively enforced in FY2026 ONLY in the Municipality of Anchorage. FNS approved Alaska's Good Faith Exemption waiver effective November 1, 2025 through October 31, 2026, exempting all Alaska census areas and boroughs from the ABAWD time-limit criteria EXCEPT the Municipality of Anchorage. Under OBBBA/H.R.1 (enacted July 4, 2025), the ABAWD age range expanded to 18-64; newly subject adults must demonstrate compliance by March 1, 2026, and the first month anyone can lose benefits for noncompliance is June 2026. Alaska Native and American Indian individuals are categorically exempt per FSP 90.
qualifying_org_definition: A public, quasi-public, or nonprofit organization (federal baseline at 7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii); no Alaska-specific narrowing found). A recipient may self-initiate community service / volunteer work at such an organization to meet the 80-hour requirement.
qualifying_org_short: Any public, quasi-public, or nonprofit organization (federal baseline; no Alaska-specific narrowing).
verification_instrument: FSP 91 (Request for ABAWD Work Program Exemption) is the primary client-facing Alaska DPA form. It asks for the recipient's name, address, case number, the name of the community service site, and supporting documentation showing hours per week. It is NOT itself an org-signed verification letter; the org-verification-letter requirement is not explicitly codified in accessible sources. No Alaska-specific dedicated 'community service hours verification' form was identified; the federal baseline self-reported statement from the qualifying org applies. DPA manual 602-1 L was inaccessible (server timeout) during both prior and current verification.
verification_form_name: FSP 91 (Request for ABAWD Work Program Exemption)
reporting_cadence_detail: The 80-hour threshold is measured per 30-day period (effectively monthly). No fixed month-end proactive-upload deadline was found; reporting is event-driven — DPA general change-reporting rules require reporting changes (including hours dropping below 20/week) within 10 days of the change.
submission_mechanism_detail: FSP 91 instructs the recipient to bring the completed form and supporting documentation to any DPA office (paper). DPA also accepts changes by fax (1-888-269-6520), email (hss.dpa.offices@alaska.gov), phone, and the Alaska Connect online portal.
submission_deadline: No specific month-end deadline for proactive ABAWD hour submissions found. FSP 91 directs the recipient to submit the completed form and documentation to any DPA office when demonstrating compliance. DPA change-reporting guidance requires reporting changes (including hours falling below 20/week) within 10 days of the change. Reporting is event-driven, not a fixed monthly upload deadline.
required_prestep_detail: No EDP, MOU, county assignment, or provider registration is required before general community-service volunteer hours count. Alaska's E&T program is 100% voluntary (all work registrants exempt from mandatory E&T per FY26 State Plan); the ABAWD general volunteer lane under 7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii) is a separate compliance path from E&T. Work registration recorded in DPA EIS is an administrative certification step, not a gating requirement. No pre-approved volunteer site list exists.
remote_stance_detail: No Alaska policy document accessible during research addresses whether remote / virtual volunteering counts. Prior file marked this 'unknown'; normalized to 'silent' (no source either permits or prohibits).
accepts_outofstate_detail: No Alaska DPA policy document (FSP 90, FSP 91, FSP 92, DPA manual 602-1 L, or FY26 E&T State Plan) restricts qualifying volunteer organizations to Alaska-based entities. The qualifying definition is simply 'public, quasi-public, or nonprofit organization' — identical to the federal baseline at 7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii). Absent any state-level restriction, the federal default applies and an out-of-state 501(c)(3) such as Tended should qualify operationally. Marked 'silent' because no source affirmatively addresses out-of-state orgs.
geographic_waivers: Good Faith Exemption waiver approved by FNS covers all Alaska census areas and boroughs EXCEPT the Municipality of Anchorage, effective November 1, 2025 through October 31, 2026. OBBBA additionally authorizes the Secretary to grant new Alaska/Hawaii good faith exemptions expiring no later than December 31, 2028 (upon a state request demonstrating good-faith effort to comply), and — on a permanent, non-expiring basis — to waive areas of Alaska/Hawaii with unemployment rates 150% above the national rate. Alaska Native and American Indian individuals are categorically exempt per FSP 90.
county_variation_detail: Implementation is statewide and uniform except for the single geographic waiver carve-out: enforcement applies in the Municipality of Anchorage and is waived elsewhere. This is a waiver-area distinction, not material county-by-county program variation in how the volunteer lane operates.
confidence_rationale: Classification (federal_80), the 80-hour requirement, the community-service volunteer lane, the FY2026 partial-enforcement / Anchorage-only waiver, the OBBBA age expansion and Alaska/Hawaii special waiver authority through Dec 31, 2028, and the FSP 91 client form are all directly corroborated by multiple sources, including the FNS-approved FY26 SNAP E&T State Plan read in full in prior research and consistent FNS/Ballotpedia/state guidance. Residual uncertainty is confined to lower-stakes fields: the exact org-verification instrument (DPA manual 602-1 L server remained inaccessible) and remote-volunteering treatment (no source addresses it). These are flagged 'silent'/unresolved and do not affect the classification or hours.
conflicts_with_prior: remote_stance: prior file used 'unknown'; normalized to 'silent' (no semantic change — no source addresses remote work).

Added explicit classification='federal_80' field and minimum-wage context ($13.00/hr eff Jan 1 2026, $14.00/hr eff Jul 1 2026); prior file did not record the state minimum wage. No change to the hours formula (still flat 80).
unresolved: Exact org-verification instrument: DPA manual 602-1 L (http://dpaweb.hss.state.ak.us/manuals/fs/602/602-1_l.htm) was inaccessible (server timeout) in both prior and current verification; no Alaska-specific dedicated community-service-hours verification form name was confirmed. Federal baseline self-reported statement from the qualifying org applies.
Remote / virtual volunteering: no Alaska policy document addresses whether it counts.
Out-of-state 501(c)(3): no source affirmatively confirms or denies; federal default appears to permit it.

Whether FSP 91 (an 'exemption request' form) is the correct vehicle for ongoing community-service compliance reporting, vs. a separate verification document — unconfirmed because the DPA manual and forms server were unreachable.
notes: VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22): Re-verified all high-stakes fields against fresh web searches and primary/official sources. The Alaska DPA web server (dpaweb.hss.state.ak.us) and the state H.R.1 impacts page (health.alaska.gov, HTTP 403 to fetcher) remained inaccessible to direct fetch this pass, consistent with prior passes; verification relied on multiple corroborating searches plus the prior pass's direct reading of the FNS-approved 97-page FY26 SNAP E&T State Plan. CONFIRMED: (1) classification federal_80 — flat 80 hrs/month (20 hrs/week) community service at a public/quasi-public/nonprofit org; Alaska does NOT operate a Workfare component, so no allotment/min-wage formula applies to the general volunteer lane. (2) enforced2026 = partial — Good Faith Exemption waiver Nov 1 2025–Oct 31 2026 exempts all AK except the Municipality of Anchorage. (3) OBBBA: age expanded 18–64 (eff. Jul 4 2025); compliance demonstration by Mar 1 2026; first benefit-loss month Jun 2026; Alaska/Hawaii special good-faith-exemption authority through Dec 31 2028 plus permanent 150%-of-national-unemployment area waiver authority. (4) No required prestep (E&T is 100% voluntary; no EDP/MOU/site-registration gate). (5) FSP 91 is the client-facing ABAWD form. (6) County variation false (only the single Anchorage waiver carve-out). Added Alaska minimum wage context ($13.00/hr eff Jan 1 2026; $14.00/hr eff Jul 1 2026 per Ballot Measure 1) for reference only — not used in the formula. | Prior-pass provenance retained below. | REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-20): Adversarial second pass attempted to refute Tier 1 classification by searching for hidden EDP requirements, pre-approved volunteer site lists, mandatory work registration gating steps, and MOU requirements for nonprofit hosts. Successfully retrieved and read the full 97-page Alaska SNAP E&T State Plan FY26 (approved 09/25/2025, FNS-approved). Key findings: (1) Alaska's E&T program is 100% voluntary — all 16,699 work registrants are exempt from mandatory E&T participation; no EDP or E&T enrollment is required before volunteer hours count under 7 CFR 273.24. (2) Alaska does not operate a Workfare component — the plan lists Workfare as an option but it is not checked. (3) The E&T plan does not describe a community service component; the ABAWD general volunteer lane under 7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii) is a separate compliance path from E&T. (4) No pre-approved volunteer site list found anywhere in the 97-page plan or any other accessible source. (5) MOU language in the plan (p. 56) applies exclusively to higher education institutions for vocational training cost parity under the E&T Vocational Training component — not to general nonprofit volunteer hosts. (6) The six E&T provider contracts (Catholic Social Services, Covenant House, Downtown Hope Center, Frontier Community Services, Goodwill, My House) are for E&T service delivery in Anchorage/Kenai/Mat-Su; a Tended nonprofit host need not appear on this list. (7) Work registration recorded in DPA EIS is an administrative certification step, not a gating requirement for ABAWD community service compliance. Zero evidence found of any hidden prestep blocking the general volunteer lane. | EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): FSP 90 (ABAWD Pamphlet, rev 12/25) directly confirms 80 hours in a 30-day period — federal_80 baseline; out-of-state nonprofit silent (no in-state restriction); geographic waivers per above; submission via any DPA office plus fax/email/phone/Alaska Connect; FSP 91 is the primary client-facing ABAWD compliance form requiring recipient name, address, case number, community service site name, and supporting documentation showing hours per week.
